2603.25537.title: Humans vs Vision-Language Models: A Unified Measure of Narrative Coherence
2603.25537.paper_summary: We study narrative coherence in visually grounded stories by comparing human-written narratives with those generated by vision-language models (VLMs) on the Visual Writing Prompts corpus. Using a set of metrics that capture different aspects of narrative coherence, including coreference, discourse relation types, topic continuity, character persistence, and multimodal character grounding, we compute a narrative coherence score. We find that VLMs show broadly similar coherence profiles that differ systematically from those of humans. In addition, differences for individual measures are often subtle, but they become clearer when considered jointly. Overall, our results indicate that, despite human-like surface fluency, model narratives exhibit systematic differences from those of humans in how they organise discourse across a visually grounded story. Our code is available at https://github.com/GU-CLASP/coherence-driven-humans.
2603.25537.paper_summary_zh: 我們透過比較人類撰寫的敘事與由視覺語言模型（VLMs）生成的敘事，來研究視覺基礎故事中的敘事一致性，這些故事來自於視覺寫作提示語料庫。<lb/>使用一組捕捉敘事一致性不同方面的指標，包括共指、話語關係類型、主題連貫性、角色持續性和多模態角色基礎，我們計算出一個敘事一致性分數。<lb/>我們發現，VLMs顯示出大致相似的連貫性特徵，但與人類的特徵系統性地不同。<lb/>此外，對於個別指標的差異通常是微妙的，但當共同考量時，這些差異變得更加明顯。<lb/>總體而言，我們的結果表明，儘管模型敘事在表面上具有類似人類的流暢性，但在如何組織視覺基礎故事中的話語方面，模型敘事與人類之間存在系統性的差異。<lb/>我們的代碼可在 https://github.com/GU-CLASP/coherence-driven-humans 獲得。
2603.25537.author: Nikolai Ilinykh et.al.
2603.25537.authors: Nikolai Ilinykh, Hyewon Jang, Shalom Lappin, Asad Sayeed, Sharid Loáiciga
